key_insight: Learning the token unmasking order in diffusion language models as a policy problem improves generation quality more reliably than fixed heuristics.
engineering_relevance: For practitioners deploying masked diffusion decoders, a lightweight learned scheduling policy can boost accuracy without changing the denoiser, making it a practical drop-in control layer.
method: Derive an upper bound on sequential decoding mismatch using KL divergence and pathwise log-likelihood to create a dense reward for order selection.
Frame unmasking order as policy optimization with a frozen denoiser and train a lightweight scheduler using Group Relative Policy Optimization.
Apply Self-Aware Scheduling to both any-order and semi-autoregressive decoding.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong gains on Sudoku (82.0% to 91.8%, and 97.5% with fine-tuning) and on reasoning tasks with LLaDA-8B, including GSM8K pass@1 rising from 64% to 76%.
watch_item: The approach adds a learned scheduling component and may depend on the quality of the bound, so performance could vary with model expressivity, generation length, and block size.
paper_title: Scheduling Thoughts: Learning the Order of Thought in Diffusion Language Models
